name: A9-DPC Parkinson disease phase 1/2a trial
summary: A single-center open-label dose-escalation phase 1/2a trial transplanting hESC-derived A9 dopamine progenitor cells into the putamen in Parkinson disease.
population: People with moderate-to-severe Parkinson disease receiving bilateral putamen A9-DPC transplantation with immunosuppression

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Stem Cell Replacement and Transplantation because they can show whether the intervention changes safety, biomarkers, and function in People with moderate-to-severe Parkinson disease receiving bilateral putamen A9-DPC transplantation with immunosuppression. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
